book_claims.claim: Regular consumption supports disease prevention and longevity
book_claims.mechanism: Part of fibre-rich food group providing vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals processed by gut microbiome into protective metabolites

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Aune et al., BMC Medicine 2016) included seeds alongside nuts, finding combined consumption associated with lower all-cause mortality, cardiovascular disease, and cancer risk in a dose-response manner.

Review (Ros & Hu, 2013; updated) found seeds provide unique lignan profiles (flax: SDG; sesame: sesamin) and essential fatty acid ratios not found in other food groups, contributing to cholesterol reduction and anti-inflammatory effects.

Systematic review found seed consumption (flax, chia, hemp, pumpkin, sunflower, sesame) associated with improved lipid profiles, reduced inflammation, and better glycemic control across multiple RCTs.

culinary_pairings.source: Probiotic + prebiotic fibre synergy; classic topping
Nutrient-dense topping adding healthy fats and crunch
Classic porridge addition for omega-3 and minerals
Seed + berry combinations for combined antioxidant profiles

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Flaxseeds: highest ALA. Hemp: balanced omega-6/omega-3 ratio. Sunflower: rich in omega-6. Chia: high ALA. Grinding improves bioavailability for flax and chia.
Gut microbiota convert plant lignans to enterolignans with anti-estrogenic and anticancer properties. Lignan type and concentration vary dramatically by seed.
Seeds are among the most mineral-dense foods per calorie. Phytic acid partially inhibits absorption; soaking or sprouting improves bioavailability.
